'''Richie Barnett''' (born {{birth date|1981|4|26|df=y}}) is a [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who has played in the 2000s and 2010s. He has played at representative level for [[Great Britain Students rugby league team|Great Britain]] ([[Student Rugby League|Students]]), and [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]], and at club level for [[Newcastle Thunder|Gateshead Thunder]] ([[semi-professional]]ly), [[Hull FC]], [[Widnes Vikings]], [[Warrington Wolves]], [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], [[Sheffield Eagles]], [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet Hawks]] (two spells), [[Keighley Cougars]] and [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]],<ref name="Statistics at donsstats.co.uk">{{cite web|url=http://www.donsstats.co.uk/player.php?PID=1166|title=Statistics at donsstats.co.uk|publisher=donsstats.co.uk|date=31 December 2018|accessdate=1 January 2019}}</ref> as a {{rlp|wg}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/>

Barnett had the best average gain per carry in the [[Warrington Wolves]] squad in 2006.

His early days were spent playing semi-professionally at [[Newcastle Thunder|Gateshead Thunder]], while he was also studying business computing at [[Leeds Metropolitan University]]. He was a key player for Leeds Metropolitan University, leading them to numerous cup and league triumphs. He was top scorer for two out of the three years he was at the university. While still at university Barnett represented Great Britain students which paved the way for Gateshead Thunder to take him on.

Barnett was sold to [[Hull F.C.]] in 2004 who paid an appearance-linked deal worth £15,000. He proved his worth by scoring 23 tries in just 25 starts, making him one of Super League's top scorers. Along the way he also picked up a Challenge Cup winners medal with Hull. He joined Warrington Wolves in 2005 and became one of the most consistent try scorers for the club.{{citation needed|date=February 2020}}

Barnett joined [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] in July 2007.{{citation needed|date=February 2020}}

In March 2008, Barnett received a two-year ban from rugby league after testing positive for [[epitestosterone]] in a drugs test the previous year.<ref>{{cite web|title=Barnett given two-year drugs ban|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7280905.stm|website=BBC Sport|accessdate=13 July 2014|date=6 March 2008}}</ref> He signed for [[Sheffield Eagles]] when returning to the game in 2010.<ref>{{cite web|title=Richie Barnett joins Sheffield Eagles|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/8516934.stm|website=BBC Sport|accessdate=13 July 2014|date=15 February 2010}}</ref>

In 2011, Barnett represented [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]] in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup Qualifiers]], where the Jamaicans took on the [[United States national rugby league team|USA]] and [[South Africa national rugby league team|South Africa]]. In 2015, Barnett played for Jamaica in their [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification – Americas|2017 Rugby League World Cup qualifiers]].{{citation needed|date=February 2020}}

He is occasionally known as Richie Barnett Jr because he used to play with an older un-related rugby league footballer, also named [[Richie Barnett]].<ref name="Sheffield sign Richie Barnett">{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleague.com/rugby-league-news/sheffield-sign-richie-barnett.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110715214502/http://www.rugbyleague.com/rugby-league-news/sheffield-sign-richie-barnett.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=15 July 2011|title=Sheffield sign Richie Barnett|publisher=rugbyleague.com|date=31 December 2011|accessdate=1 January 2012}}</ref>
